summary: Finished matching the game-mode source file src/melee/gm/gm_17AD.c and flipped it from NonMatching to Matching in configure.py. The key code change was correcting MatchPlayerData kill fields from separate scalar members into a u16 kills[4] array, then updating gm_17AD access patterns to index that array. Header cleanup also reduced dependency on gmresult.static.h by forward-declaring ResultsPlayerData and adding needed matrix/GX-related includes.
what_changed: Changed configure.py so Object(NonMatching, "melee/gm/gm_17AD.c") became Object(Matching, "melee/gm/gm_17AD.c").
Updated gm_17AD.c includes from local/placeholder-style includes to canonical angle-bracket includes for <melee/gm/gmresult.h>, <melee/gm/types.h>, and <melee/lb/lblanguage.h>.
Replaced accesses to MatchPlayerData fields kills, x12, x14, and x16 with indexed accesses through kills[0], kills[1], kills[2], and kills[3].

Fixed several functions in gm_17AD.c to index kills by the argument or by the target player slot, including fn_8017AE70, fn_8017AED8, fn_8017AF40, fn_8017AFA8, fn_8017B07C, fn_8017B0E4, fn_8017B14C, and fn_8017B1B4.
Changed struct MatchPlayerData in src/melee/gm/types.h from four adjacent u16 fields named kills, x12, x14, and x16 into one u16 kills[4] array.
Adjusted gmresult.h dependencies by removing inclusion of gm/gmresult.static.h, adding <dolphin/mtx.h>, and forward-declaring struct ResultsPlayerData.
smart_moves: Recognized that four adjacent u16 fields in MatchPlayerData were semantically one indexed kill-count array, which let the compiler generate the expected indexed access patterns.
Used the struct layout improvement to simplify several nearly identical result-accessor functions without changing the underlying offsets.
Promoted gm_17AD.c to Matching only after the source and header layout changes aligned enough to match.
Reduced header coupling in gmresult.h by replacing a static-header include with a forward declaration where the diff evidence shows only the type name was needed.
decomp_lessons: When several adjacent unknown fields are accessed with variable indexing in assembly, model them as an array rather than separate scalar fields.
A struct-layout correction can be the decisive fix for matching a whole translation unit, especially when nonmatching code has repeated offset-specific field names like x12, x14, and x16.
Preserve layout while improving names: replacing four u16 fields with u16 kills[4] keeps the byte footprint equivalent but exposes the compiler-friendly access form.
For headers, prefer forward declarations over including large/static headers when only an incomplete type is required; this can reduce dependency churn while keeping declarations valid.
Matching status changes should be reflected in configure.py by moving the relevant Object from NonMatching to Matching.
naming_conventions: The PR converted anonymous offset-style fields x12, x14, and x16 into semantic array elements under kills[4].
The field name kills was retained as the meaningful base name for the array of per-player or per-slot kill counts.
Unidentified functions remained in address-derived form such as fn_8017AE70 and fn_8017B1B4; the PR did not provide stronger evidence for semantic function names.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Use arrays in struct definitions to induce indexed load patterns instead of hard-coded scalar offset accesses.
For repeated accessor functions, ensure the index dimension is represented correctly: some functions index player_standings[arg0].kills[N], while others index player_standings[N].kills[arg0].
Keep #pragma dont_inline on where existing source notes indicate inlining control is needed for matching; the PR did not remove the TODO about forcing other functions not to inline the helper.
Small formatting/control-flow cleanup, such as combining an else with a closing brace, accompanied the matching change but the main matching tactic was the data-layout correction.
review_feedback: No review comments were present in the provided PR slice.
No explicit PR body or human rationale was available beyond the title and diff; conclusions are based on the changed files and diff excerpt.
follow_up_queries: Look for later PRs that rename fn_8017AE70 through fn_8017B1B4 after gm_17AD became matching.
Search for other MatchPlayerData offset fields near x18, x1C, and x20 that may also become semantic arrays or named result-stat fields.
Check whether gmresult.h continued to avoid gmresult.static.h after this PR or if later header dependency changes refined the ResultsPlayerData declaration.
Find other matching PRs where adjacent scalar fields were converted into arrays to fix indexed access codegen.
